element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core tool throughout; uncharged shots are consistent baseline
Players learn timing and resource management; charged shots deal more damage

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early levels (convention from previous Mega Man games)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Mobility and evasion in platforming; traversal optimization

element_introduction_order.element: First four Robot Masters (Freeze Man, Junk Man, Burst Man, Cloud Man)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each defeated master grants a unique Master Weapon with specific use cases
Each weapon weak-matches against other Robot Masters; encourages experimentation with order

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from start; used in levels
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Jumping aid and utility tasks; extended platforming capabilities
Reveals hidden items; encourages thorough level exploration
Collecting all four unlocks Rush Super Adaptor; end-game power-up

element_introduction_order.element: Second four Robot Masters (Spring Man, Slash Man, Shade Man, Turbo Man)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Unlocked after defeating first four masters
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progression gate; increased difficulty and complexity
Powerful offensive and mobility tool for late-game stages; combines Mega Man 6 enhancements
Used at Eddie's/Auto's shop; enables purchase of consumables and power-ups

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Accessible from stage select after introduction
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Trading system for bolts; risk-reward decisions on power-up purchases

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Obtainable as special item during progression
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Defensive capability; adds strategic depth to combat

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Obtainable as special item during progression
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Offensive companion; adds attack variation (originally unimplemented in final build)
Mid-game antagonists; reveal themselves as Wily's agents; mirror-match difficulty
Ultimate challenge; multiple phases; employs saucer escape vehicle

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching a safe platform or checkpoint
typical_tension_triggers: Bottomless pits with no safety net
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Stage-gated progression with medium intensity; four robot masters unlock access to four more masters, creating mid-game gate. Early stages are tutorial-paced; mid-game complexity ramps with elemental obstacles matching weapon types; late-game compression with harder platforming and multi-stage final confrontation.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Off-path platforming branches contain R-U-S-H letters and Rush Search dig spots; non-critical exploration rewards
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden letters spelled R-U-S-H throughout stages signal optional collection path to super upgrade

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-friendly with basic platforming and four accessible Robot Masters; charging mechanics and slide movement established; Rush companion introduced
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Difficulty spike after first four Robot Masters defeated; remaining four masters introduce stage hazards matching their elemental weapons (ice platforms, junk obstacles, explosive timing, cloud wind effects); elemental puzzle-solving begins
difficulty_curve.late_game: Compressed difficulty with Bass/Treble encounters and Wily's multi-stage fortress; requires mastery of weapon matchups; Rush Super Adaptor becomes key tool; final boss escalates complexity
notable_spikes: Transition from first four to second four Robot Masters (progression gate + difficulty jump)
Bass/Treble mid-game encounters (skill check; no stage context)
Wily's fortress stages (chained platforming + saucer escape sequence)

notable_level_patterns.description: Non-standard introduction; establishes narrative; minimal combat; introduces characters (Roll, Auto); leads directly to stage select. Unused Bunby Met animations suggest this was expanded in prototype.

Selectable from start. Feature distinct elemental hazards: Freeze Man (ice platforms), Junk Man (rotating drums, junk obstacles), Burst Man (explosive timing), Cloud Man (wind effect platforming). Medium difficulty baseline.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Freeze Man / Junk Man / Burst Man / Cloud Man stages

notable_level_patterns.description: Unlocked after defeating first four. Increased complexity: Spring Man (spring mechanics), Slash Man (sword hazards), Shade Man (petrification gimmick), Turbo Man (speed platforming). Requires weapon matchups from first set.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Spring Man / Slash Man / Shade Man / Turbo Man stages

notable_level_patterns.description: Chained progression; no stage select. Multi-stage sequences requiring accumulated weapons and tools. Includes Bass/Treble confrontations. Wily Saucer escape sequence (unused larger animation variants suggest prototype had different structure).
Hidden R-U-S-H letter locations encourage branch exploration. Rush Search dig spots provide consumables. Non-critical paths reward thorough players without mandatory backtracking.

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Mega Man 7)
